items.headline: U.S. Stocks Retreat from Records Amid Surging Oil Prices and Tech Sell-Off
items.summary: The U.S. stock market experienced a broad-based decline on Friday, May 15, 2026, as major indices pulled back from recent record highs. The downturn was primarily driven by a surge in oil prices, climbing Treasury yields, and significant profit-taking in the technology sector, particularly among AI-related stocks. Overall market sentiment turned cautious amid inflation concerns and a re-evaluation of high valuations.

items.headline: Microsoft Rises on Investor Position
items.summary: Microsoft bucked the broader tech sell-off, climbing after billionaire investor Bill Ackman's Pershing Square Capital Management revealed a significant new position in the software giant.

items.headline: Cisco Systems Continues Upward Trend
items.summary: Cisco Systems recorded gains, reaching a 52-week high, amidst a generally down market.

items.headline: Nvidia Leads Tech Sector Decline
items.summary: Nvidia, a key beneficiary of the AI revolution, was the heaviest weight on the S&P 500, dropping sharply as investors took profits from the recent rally in technology stocks.

items.headline: Micron Technology Falls Amid Profit-Taking
items.summary: Micron Technology was another significant decliner, falling as investors pulled back from high-performing semiconductor stocks.

items.headline: Intel Experiences Sharp Decline
items.summary: Intel shares fell more than 6% as the technology sector saw widespread profit-taking.

items.headline: Tesla Shares Drop
items.summary: Tesla's stock saw a notable decline, contributing to the broader market's cautious sentiment.

items.headline: Energy Sector Outperforms Amid Rising Oil Prices
items.summary: The energy sector was a notable outlier, advancing by 2.32% on the S&P 500, benefiting from surging crude oil prices. West Texas Intermediate crude for June delivery rose 4.2% to settle at $105.42 a barrel.

items.headline: Technology Sector Leads Market Sell-Off
items.summary: Technology stocks experienced widespread profit-taking, leading declines across the market. The sector's downturn was a sharp turnaround after a meteoric rise for much of the year, driven by artificial intelligence euphoria.

items.headline: Materials and Utilities Among Lagging Sectors
items.summary: Beyond technology, the materials and utilities sectors were also significant underperformers, dropping 2.74% and 2.4% respectively on the S&P 500.

items.headline: Consumer Cyclical and Real Estate Face Headwinds
items.summary: The consumer cyclical sector fell 3.29%, and real estate declined by 2.89%, indicating broader weakness beyond the tech-heavy segments.

items.headline: Kevin Warsh Confirmed as New Fed Chair; Powell Serves Interim
items.summary: The Senate confirmed Kevin Warsh to a 14-year term as a Fed governor and as the new Federal Reserve Chair. Jerome Powell will serve as interim Fed chair until Warsh is sworn in, as Powell's second four-year term officially ended on Friday.

items.headline: Treasury Yields Climb on Inflation Concerns
items.summary: Treasury yields continued to rise, with the 10-year Treasury note yield increasing to 4.59% from 4.47% and the 30-year Treasury bond yield topping 5.1%. This movement reflects growing inflation worries and increased expectations of a potential Fed interest rate hike later in the year, with a nearly 51% probability priced in for December.

items.headline: Oil Prices Surge, Rattling Bond Market
items.summary: Global oil prices surged, with Brent crude rising 3.3% to settle at $109.26 per barrel. This increase contributed to inflation fears and put pressure on the bond market, adding to the day's market downturn.

items.headline: Mixed Economic Data Released
items.summary: U.S. industrial production improved more than economists expected, and manufacturing in New York state showed expansion. However, U.S. unemployment claims rose to 211,000 in early May 2026, missing market expectations. U.S. retail sales in April rose 0.5% month-over-month and 4.9% year-over-year, indicating continued consumer spending.

items.headline: Applied Materials Beats Q2 Expectations, Strong Outlook
items.summary: Applied Materials ($AMAT) reported stronger-than-expected Q2 earnings, with EPS of $2.86 beating by $0.18 and revenue of $7.91 billion exceeding estimates by $219.16 million. The company provided an optimistic Q3 revenue forecast of $8.95 billion and non-GAAP EPS of $3.36, projecting over 30% growth in semiconductor equipment for 2026.

items.headline: Jackson Financial's Q1 Earnings Miss Forecasts, Market Reacts Favorably
items.summary: Jackson Financial ($JFN) reported first-quarter revenue of $2.90 billion, a 22.6% decline year-over-year, and adjusted EPS of $5.15, missing analyst estimates. Despite the misses, the market reacted favorably due to strong demand for retail annuity products, particularly Registered Index-Linked Annuities (RILAs) and Fixed Indexed Annuities (FIAs).
